titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Harry Crane Papers PASC.0203
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Julie Graham, K. Bachli, C. Butler, and N. Schneider; machine-readable finding aid created by Julie Graham.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 May 27.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-27 14:36:36 -0700.

did.abstract: Comedic writer Harry Crane started his career as a standup comic. During his long career Crane wrote for a number of renowned entertainers, but is best remembered for developing the popular television creation, The Honeymooners. The collection consists of script files, gag files, humor topical publications, and a small amount of awards, audio visual material, books, business files, clippings, correspondence, memorabilia, and photographs related to Cranes' prolific career writing for radio, television, and motion pictures as well as his long association with popular entertainers.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

Portions of collection unprocessed. Material in the backlog series is unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: This collection contains both processed and unprocessed audiovisual materials. Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access, unless otherwise noted in a Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements note at the series and file levels. All requests to access processed digital materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Provenance/Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Barbara Gilbert Cowan and Stephanie Crane Hirsh, 2000.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Harry Crane Papers (Collection PASC 203). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Julie Graham, K. Bachli, C. Butler, and N. Schneider.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Potentially Offensive Description in Library Special Collections.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Harry Kravitsky (later changed to Crane) was born in 1914 in Brooklyn, New York. He started his show business career playing kiddy shows as a teenager, becoming a standup comedian by age 19. His radio writing career spanned the 1930s through to the early 1950s working on a variety of programs such as Abbott and Costello, The Jack Caron Show, Blue Ribbon Town, The Danny Thomas Show, The Jack Carson Show, The Jimmy Durante Show, The Joan Davis Show, and Songs for Sale, among others. In the 1940s, he was recruited by MGM to write Hollywood films earning his first credit with Air Raid Wardens (1943). During his eight year association with MGM he continued writing for radio and adding to his screenwriting credits with The Harvey Girls (1946), Lost in a Harem (1944), Two Sisters from Boston (1946), and Ziegfeld Follies (1946), among others. As a screen writer, Crane developed his skill for characterization and comedy construction that served him throughout his career.

Crane's television career started early in the 1950s developing the Jan Murray Songs for Sale program. From there, he went on to be involved in a number of popular variety shows. He also began his association with Jackie Gleason in the early 1950s working on the television program Cavalcade of Stars. Gleason asked Crane and Joe Bigelow, another staff writer, to help develop a sketch about a working-class man living in a small Brooklyn flat which developed into the Honeymooners. The popular Honeymooner's segment moved to CBS as part of The Jackie Gleason Show and eventually became a half-hour situation comedy during the 1955-56 television season. Crane was also the principal writer for the Gleason variety show, thus creating several of Jackie Gleason's other memorable characters. Crane developed a long working relation with Dean Martin and Jerry Lewis on The Colgate Comedy Hour. He continued to work with Martin in later years on various projects among them The Dean Martin Celebrity Roasts, The Dean Martin Comedy Hour, and The Dean Martin Show. Crane also had a long record of scripting award presentation shows, among them the Academy Awards (1957 and 1959), the Golden Globes (1968), and the Emmy Awards (1968, 1971, 1973, 1974). He wrote a special Oscar tribute to Jackie Gleason for the Academy Awards presentation in 1987.

In addition to his many motion picture, radio, and television project credits, during his career Crane wrote for a number of renowned entertainers such as Milton Berle, Joey Bishop, Bing Crosby, Jimmy Durante, Alan King, Steve Lawrence and Eydie Gorme, Frank Sinatra, Danny Thomas and Andy Williams, among others. Crane even wrote humorous material for national politicians. Over the years, he wrote for and befriended a variety of entertainers and at the same time he encouraged and boosted the careers of younger contemporaries such as Mel Brooks and Neil Simon. Crane continued to make valuable contributions to the entertainment industry providing comedic as well as serious and dramatic material to Hollywood's elite until his death in Los Angeles, California, on September 13, 1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of material related to the career of comedic writer Harry Crane. The collection includes script files, gag files, humor topical publications, and a small amount of awards, audio visual material, books, business files, clippings, correspondence, memorabilia, and photographs documenting Crane's prolific career writing for radio, television, motion pictures and special appearance performance projects.

The script files includes various drafts of complete and partial scripts, monologues, and other writings such as sketches, bits, and inserts, among others, for a variety of radio, television, and motion pictures projects. In addition, there are writings for special/live performances for a number of well-known entertainers. The radio writings include material for popular programs such as The Abbott and Costello Show, Blue Ribbon Town, The Jimmy Durante Show, The Edgar Bergen Show, The Danny Thomas Show, The Joan Davis Show, The Jack Carson Show, and Songs for Sale, among many others. The television writings comprise materials for a number of popular television variety shows such as Academy and Emmy award shows, The Andy Williams Show, the Colgate Comedy Hour, numerous Dean Martin show incarnations including Dean Martin celebrity roasts, The Frank Sinatra Show, The Joey Bishop Show, and Perry Como specials, among others. Of particular note are Crane's original writings for Jackie Gleason and The Honeymooners, which includes original bits, ideas, and script material. The motion picture writings include material for Air Warden and Harvey Girls, among other endeavors. The special appearances writings reflect Crane's long association with a number of entertainers including Paul Anka, Steve Lawrence and Eydie Gorme, Dean Martin, Frank Sinatra, Danny Thomas, and Andy Williams, among others. There is also a small amount of unique writings that appear to be authored by Crane and written specifically for friends' personal events or activities. In addition there are a number of unidentified project files that contain a mixture of bits, ideas, inserts, monologues, saves, sketches, and scripts.

The small amount of business files contain fragmentary records of business and financial dealings related to Crane's career and include Crane's perseverance regarding credits and/or residual compensation associated with The Honeymooners or Jackie Gleason related creations. Crane's gag file includes a topical based mix of one liners and jokes compiled by Crane (it is not certain how many are his original ideas). The humor topical publications include a compilation of topical jokes, skits, and comedic lines for comedic professionals. Among the publications are Fun-Master Monthly's The Comedian and Orben's Current Comedy among others. The small library of hardback and soft back books collected by Crane comprise a mix of joke books, books about the Honeymooners, Jackie Gleason, the entertainment industry, and other assorted topics.

The correspondence consists of a small number of brief letters and/or notes from friends or business associates to Crane and of particular note are original thank you notes from Senator Hubert Humphrey and Senator John F. Kennedy. The photographs are a mixture of color and black and white images reflecting Crane's personal and professional life and include candid and publicity photographs of Crane by himself and/or with family, friends, and/or colleagues. Among those pictured are Barbara, Stephanie, Lillian and Harry Crane, Melissa and Sarah Gilbert, Milton Berle, Steve Allen and Jayne Meadows, Jerry Lewis, Dean Martin, Jackie Gleason, and Andy Williams among others.

The small quantity of audio and video tapes, awards, and printed materials in the collection further document Crane's many associations and long career in the entertainment industry. The bulk of the audio visual material contains "home" audio and video recordings collected by Crane and appear to be related to his career. The small number of items documenting awards and honors bestowed upon Crane during his career include laminated and original certificates from the Academy of Motion Pictures Arts and Sciences and the Academy of Television Arts and Sciences. Printed materials are mainly a mix of newspaper and magazine clippings collected by Crane that are about Crane, projects in which he was involved, his family and/or his colleagues, and/or other topics of interest such as comedy.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p:
Arranged in the following series:
Series 1. Motion Picture Projects
Series 2. Radio Projects
Series 3. Special Appearances
Series 4. Television Projects
Series 5. Awards
Series 6. Audio Visual Materials
Series 7. Baseball Memorabilia
Series 8. Books
Series 9. Business Files
Series 10. Correspondence
Series 12. Crane Family
Series 13. Gag Files
Series 14. Harry Crane Writings
Series 15. Humor Topicals
Series 16. Music
Series 17. Personal and Biographical
Series 18. Photographs
Series 19. Printed Material
Series 20. Unidentified Projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Motion Picture Projects include script material for a small number of projects for which it is believed Crane was involved in some capacity. The script material is a mixture of complete and partial scripts, sketches, and notes. Of particular note is a script for Air Warden (1943), a partial script for Harvey Girls (1946), and script material for Ziegfeld Follies (1946), all of which are among Crane's early motion picture writing credits. Additionally, there is script material for Dear God and Jumbo, among others, for which it is not known in what capacity Crane was involved. The script files are arranged alphabetically by project titles. See also the Unidentified Projects Series for possible additional material related to motion picture writing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Radio Projects series includes script material for a number of projects for which it is believed Crane was involved in some capacity. The series is arranged alphabetically into subseries by known program or project titles. The writings include a mixture of complete and partial scripts, sketches, segments, bits, inserts, and ideas for radio programs such as The Abbott and Costello Show, Blue Ribbon Town, The Jimmy Durante Show, The Edgar Bergen Show, The Danny Thomas Show, The Joan Davis Show, The Jack Carson Show, and Songs for Sale, among many others. The files within each subseries are arranged alphabetically by file titles which reflect the content of the folders. A portion of the file titles were created or enhanced by the processor. Crane originally filed a portion of the radio scripts in loose bound binders, which appeared to be collated together by specific entertainers and/or by radio program title. These scripts were removed from the binders for preservation purposes and the notebook labels noted in the notes' field of the container list. See also the Unidentified Projects Series for possible additional material related to radio writing.

Xmas-Santa Claus - material written for Jimmy Durante and Bob Hope
Boy-girl - material written for Jimmy Durante, Jack Carson, and Linda Darnell
English-boy-girl-society - material written for [Claudette] Colbert and Jimmy Durante

Music - material written for Louis Armstrong, Jimmy Durante, Paulette Goddard, and Johnny Mercer
Newspaper-boy-girl-society-ballet-music - material written for George Murphy, Ransom Sherman, Mel Blanc, Linda Darnell, Betty Rhodes, and Jimmy Durante

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Annotated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Special Appearances series is a mixture of script and monologue material, bits, inserts, and file notes which reflect Crane's long association with a number of entertainers including Paul Anka, Steve Lawrence and Eydie Gorme, Dean Martin, Frank Sinatra, Danny Thomas, and Andy Williams, among others. It appears the writings were created for individual entertainers for an assortment of traditional and nontraditional live or special appearances and/or events ranging from night club appearances to dinner engagements. Additionally there are a small number of items for Scopus Awards'events for which Crane may have been a creative consultant. The series is arranged alphabetically into subseries by names or projects. The files within each subseries are arranged alphabetically by file titles reflecting the contents of each folder. See also the Unidentified Projects Series for possible additional material related to special appearances.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Television Projects series includes script material for a number of projects for which it is believed Crane was involved in some capacity. The series is arranged alphabetically into subseries by known program or project titles or name. For unidentified television material, the processor created the subseries, Unidentified Projects. The writings include a mixture of complete and partial scripts, sketches, segments, bits, inserts, saves, file notes, ideas, and occasional production information for a number of popular television variety shows including Academy and Emmy Awards shows, the Andy Williams Show, the Colgate Comedy Hour, numerous Dean Martin show incarnations including Dean Martin celebrity roasts, The Frank Sinatra Show, The Honeymooners, The Joey Bishop Show, and Perry Como specials, among others. The files within each subseries are arranged alphabetically by title, entertainer name, and/or writing format. A portion of the file titles were created by or enhanced by the processor. Of particular note in the television project series are Crane's original writings for Jackie Gleason and The Honeymooners, including pieces for Gleason characters Joe the Bartender, Fenwick Babbit, The Loudmouth, and Honeymooner scripts. Additionally, there is a significant body of material related to Dean Martin 'celebrity roasts', including monologues and rebuttals for the roasts. As the roasts were produced under various television program titles such as Dean Martin Comedy Roasts and Dean Martin Celebrity Roasts, they are arranged into subseries, Dean Martin Roasts followed by the name of the honoree. Crane originally filed a portion of the television scripts in loose bound binders which appeared to be collated together by individual entertainer. These scripts were removed from the binders for preservation purposes and the notebook labels are noted in the notes' field of the container list. See also the Unidentified Projects Series for possible additional material related to television writing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Awards series include a small amount of material documenting awards and honors bestowed upon Crane during his career. Included are laminated and original certificates from the Academy of Motion Pictures Arts and Sciences for service to the Academy; and the Academy of Television Arts and Sciences for his contributions to the Dean Martin Show and Andy Williams Show. Additionally, there is a Christopher award presented to Crane in 1980. There is no specific arrangement to the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Audio Visual Materials series comprises is a small amount of sound recordings and video tapes collected by Crane and which appear to be related to his career. The bulk of the material are "home" audio and video recordings along with a small number of commercially produced video recordings. Included are items related to Crane's association with Jimmy Durante, Jackie Gleason, Alan King, Martin and Lewis, and Andy Williams, among others. The series is arranged into subseries by format; each subseries is arranged alphabetically by item title. Access to the series is restricted; use of audio material requires production of listening or viewing copies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Business Files series contain a small amount of fragmentary records documenting business and financial dealings related to Crane's career. Included is a mixture of contracts, correspondence, file notes, and assorted payment records. The bulk of the information in the files reflects Crane's awareness regarding credits and/or residual compensation associated with the use (mostly re-runs) of The Honeymooners or Jackie Gleason related material, starting around the 1980s. The files are arranged alphabetically by file titles reflecting the contents of each folder. A portion of the file titles were enhanced by the processor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Correspondence series consists of a small number of brief letters and/or notes from friends or business associates to Crane. The communications appear to be personal in nature, but may be related to professional interactions. Correspondents include Jerry Lewis, Garry Marshall, and Suzanne Sommers. Of particular note are original thank you notes from Senator Hubert Humphrey and from Senator John F. Kennedy. The files are arranged alphabetically by correspondent's name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Crane Family series contains a very small mixture of keepsakes collected by Crane related to his children and grandchildren. Included are cards, handmade notes and drawings, among other items. The files are arranged by family member name or topic. See also the Printed Materials Series and Photographs Series for material related to Crane's family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Gag Files series comprises Crane's unique compilation of a topical based mix of one liners and jokes (it is unknown how much of the material in the files are Cranes original ideas). The files are arranged alphabetically by theme areas created by Crane such as Accidents, Actors, Bathing Suits, Music, Nudists, Off Color, Old Maids, Séances, Superstitious, Woman and You Tell Em, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Harry Crane Writings series contains a small amount of unique writings that appear to be authored by Crane and written specifically for friends' personal events or activities. Included are jokes, notes, and other reflections written for individuals such as Sammy Cahn, Dean Martin, and Danny Thomas, among others. The files are arranged alphabetically by names or projects. The file titles were enhanced by the processor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Humor Topicals series comprise a small and unique library of serial-like publications aimed specifically to comedic professionals. They contain a compilation of topical jokes, skits, and comedic lines and include Fun-Master Monthly The Comedian and Orben's Current Comedy among others. Some of the issues are annotated. The series is arranged alphabetically into subseries by publication titles. The processor created the subseries, Assorted Publications for one offs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Memorabilia Series contains a very small mix of posters, programs, and ephemera collected by Crane. It is not clear how the posters or programs are related to Crane. Included with the ephemera are items from Crane's 60th birthday and blank NBC stationary. The series has no specific arrangement.

scopecontent.p: Empire, Shubert, Gayety Theatre, Palace on Broadway, and Daly's Theatre.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Music Series contains a small amount of published sheet music, songbooks, and lesson books collected by Crane. The sheet music and songbooks are mostly vocal selections from what appears to be popular stage productions by known composers such as Cole Porter, Irving Berlin, and George Gershwin, among others. The lesson books are for piano and violin. It is unknown how Crane used the material. The series is arranged alphabetically by publication title or by file titles reflecting the contents of each folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This series contains a few items documenting Cranes personal life. Included in this series is a copy of Cranes birth certificate, a junior high school report card, and a marriage certificate recording his marriage to wife Lillian Crane. There is no specific arrangement to the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Photograph Series are a mixture of color and black and white images reflecting Crane's personal and professional life. The photographs are arranged alphabetically into five subseries: Suberies 1. Harry Crane; Subseries 2. Baseball; Subseries 3. Family; Subseries 4. People and show business; Subseries 5. Social gatherings and trips. Included in the series are candid and publicity photographs of Crane by himself and/or with family, friends, and/or colleagues. Additionally, there are a small number of photographs that do not include Crane. The photographs give a glimpse of Crane's lengthy show business career relationships, document social gatherings including Harry Crane birthday celebrations, unknown social events and trips, and give proof to his passion for baseball. Additionally, there are a small number of photographs of Crane's family. Among those pictured are Barbara, Stephanie, Lillian and Harry Crane, Melissa and Sarah Gilbert, Milton Berle, Steve Allen and Jayne Meadows, Jerry Lewis, Dean Martin, Jackie Gleason, and Andy Williams among others. Most of the photographs are not dated, but it is likely they represent the 1940s-1980s. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by file titles that reflect the contents of the folders. Many of the file titles were created or enhanced by processor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Printed Materials series consist of clippings, articles, and printed memorabilia collected by Crane. The series is arranged alphabetically into two subseries: Subseries 1. Articles; and Subseries 2. Publicity. Subseries 1., Articles, are a mixture of newspaper and magazine pieces collected by Crane that cover a variety of topics including comedy, show business, and quotes. Subseries 2, Publicity, are a small mixture of clippings, tear outs, and magazine articles about or with mention of Crane or projects in which Crane was involved. Additionally there are a very small number of items related to family and/and or colleagues. The bulk of the clippings were found together in a container labeled "publicity." The files in each subseries are arranged alphabetically by file titles enhanced or supplied by the processor, which reflect the contents of each folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Unidentified Projects series contains a mixture of bits, ideas, inserts, monologues, saves, sketches, and scripts with no project or date information. The series is arranged into subseries chronologically by decades. For files with no date, the processor created the subseries, No date. The files within each subseries are arranged alphabetically by file titles, which reflect the contents of each folder. A portion of file titles were enhanced or created by the processor (some of which includes the addition of an entertainer's name, believed to be affiliated with or intended for the piece).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to Harry Crane's career. Includes script material, monologues, dialogues, photographs, notes, humor topicals, and a modest amount of correspondence, videos, books, awards, and ephemera.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Unprocessed series. This material is unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections.
